reading.label: B
paragraphs: Sara has a small *business*. She makes bags at home. She sews them by hand. Every bag is different and pretty. Sara wants to sell her bags. She does not have a *shop*. So she sells them on the *internet*. She takes photos of each bag. Then she puts them on a website with a *price*.

At first, Sara had only one *customer* a week. She felt sad. But she did not stop. She made better photos. She wrote a nice *advert* for each bag. Slowly, more people came. Now she sells ten bags a week. She makes a small *profit* from every bag. Sara saves this money in the bank.

Sara knows that a business has some *risk*. Maybe a bag does not sell. Maybe a customer is not happy. But Sara is not afraid. She likes her small *idea*. She loves to make bags and meet new people online. Her dream is to open a real shop one day.

questions.question_en: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: A woman who sells handmade bags online
A man who opens a coffee shop
A big shop in the city
questions.correct_answer: A woman who sells handmade bags online
questions.explanation_ar: النص عن سارة التي تبيع حقائب يدوية الصنع عبر الإنترنت.

questions.question_en: Where does Sara sell her bags?
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنها تبيع حقائبها على الإنترنت لأنها لا تملك محلاً.

questions.question_en: How many bags does Sara sell now each week?
questions.question_ar: كم حقيبة تبيع سارة الآن كل أسبوع؟
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إنها الآن تبيع عشر حقائب في الأسبوع.

questions.question_en: In the passage, what does the word "profit" mean?
choices: The money you keep after costs
questions.correct_answer: The money you keep after costs
questions.explanation_ar: كلمة profit تعني الربح، أي المال الذي يبقى بعد دفع التكاليف.

questions.question_en: What can we understand about Sara?
questions.question_ar: ماذا نفهم عن سارة من النص؟
choices: She works hard and does not give up
She is lazy and stops quickly
questions.correct_answer: She works hard and does not give up
questions.explanation_ar: رغم أن البداية كانت صعبة، لم تتوقف سارة بل عملت بجد، فنفهم أنها لا تستسلم.

questions.question_en: What happened after Sara made better photos and wrote nice adverts?
questions.question_ar: ماذا حدث بعد أن صنعت سارة صوراً أفضل وكتبت إعلانات جميلة؟
questions.explanation_ar: بسبب الصور الأفضل والإعلانات الجميلة، جاء المزيد من الناس للشراء.

questions.question_en: What does Sara do first with each bag?
questions.question_ar: ما أول شيء تفعله سارة مع كل حقيبة؟
choices: She saves money in the bank
questions.explanation_ar: قبل أن تبيع الحقيبة، تلتقط سارة صوراً لها ثم تضعها على الموقع بسعر.

vocab: the work of making, buying, or selling things
She started a small business at home.
a place where you buy things
He wants to open a shop.
the system that links computers around the world
She sells bags on the internet.
The price of the bag is low.
a message that helps sell something
She wrote a short advert for each bag.
the money you keep after you pay costs
She makes a small profit from every bag.
the chance that something bad can happen
a plan or thought in your mind
made by hand, not by a machine
Her bags are handmade and pretty.

listening.transcript: Hi, I'm Omar. I have a small food cart near the university. I sell hot sandwiches and juice. I make everything fresh every morning. My prices are low, so students love my cart. I have many customers now. I make a small profit each day. One day, I want to open a real restaurant.

exercises.question_en: Why do students love Omar's cart?
exercises.explanation_ar: الطلاب يحبون عربته لأن أسعاره منخفضة.
يحلم بأن يفتح مطعماً حقيقياً يوماً ما.

exercises.question_en: What can we understand about Omar's business?
exercises.explanation_ar: لديه زبائن كثيرون ويحقق ربحاً صغيراً كل يوم، لذلك نفهم أن مشروعه يسير بشكل جيد.
